What time is it? This is the вҖҳNuit Fantastique Grained HavanaвҖҷ watch by Trilobe, a brand that has broken the norms of time-telling. Mercury is the closest planet from the Sun. The side facing the sun is extremely hot, whereas the other side is incredibly cold. The temperatures range from -150 C to 425. Without your spacesuit, youвҖҷd either freeze or instantly turn into a carbon brick, depending on which side of the planet you were standing on.Without any gear, you would survive for less than 2 minutes.вң©VenusIt is a real hell.The temperature on its surface is approximately 400 C. Its surface pressure is also about 90 times greater than that of Earth. A spacesuit designed for Venus would need to be constructed of titanium. Without a spacesuit, one would get squashed in seconds, if you didnвҖҷt get turned into dust.вң©Earth:Thanks to the wonderful atmosphere, food and water, and everything else that makes our home planet liveable, you can get in a good 80 or more years here. Earth is perfect for us.вң©MarsIt is very cold, but the atmosphere is very thin вҖҳTyson says, вҖңso the intensity of the cold wonвҖҷt be as biting as it might otherwise feel at that temperature on Earth.вҖқвң© JupiterJupiter is literally just a ball of gas with a solid core. So thereвҖҷs nothing to land on. If you were to jump on it, you would just descend into nothingness. The gases inside this giant are highly pressurized, so without any gear, you would get crushed instantly, i.e., in less than 1 second.вң°вң°вң° Just like Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune are enormous balls of gas. вҖңI was a 28-year-old happy man, who would go to work in the morning & eat Maa ke haath ka roti in the evening. It was just Papa, Maa, & me. But covid snatched it all. It was 2020. First, I lost my job & thenвҖҰI lost my parents. The next few months after their death were the toughest. I didnвҖҷt go anywhere, talk to anyone or eat anything. I remained within the 4 walls of my house. I fell into depression.I couldnвҖҷt stay in that house anymore. So, the moment covid got over, I shifted to a PG. I took up multiple jobs. In the morning I sold pamphlets & in the evening I worked as a waiter. But at night all the memories would come flashing back. I would often wake up to wet pillows.Thankfully, thatвҖҷs when I came across a video of artists performing on the streets. This intrigued me & I saw the maximum crowd among the mascots. This way I could be closer to people. So, I decided to give it a try. I spent hours searching for the perfect outfit onlineвҖҰ& I decided to become a вҖҳTeddy.вҖҷMany people asked me, вҖҳWhy Teddy?вҖҷ But the 1 thing I needed the most at the time was a warm hugвҖҰ& isnвҖҷt that what teddies do? Give the best hugs? I knew what I was doing was completely insane, but I just went with my gut feeling.So, in 2020 I beganвҖҰit was difficult to breathe & the heat gave me rashes & allergies. But 1 day, a kid hugged me & said, вҖҳHi Teddy Man. Aapko pata hai aaj school mei kya hua?вҖҷ And then he narrated everything that happened to him in school. That day, I became the вҖҳTeddy Man.вҖҷItвҖҷs been 4 years since. Every day I meet multiple kids, some give me a fist bump, some call me the вҖҳYellow ManвҖҷ & some just talk. In between all this I have healed. ItвҖҷs not like I havenвҖҷt had bad days. There were days when drunk men have slapped me & run away with my money,But I live for the days when an old uncle sits & tells me about his day, or when a foreigner clicks a photo with me because itвҖҷs her first time in Bombay, or simply when a passerby gives me his bottle of water.This 1 avatar has given me a 2nd chance at life. IвҖҷm doing this for myselfвҖҰbecause everyday, this job is giving me the family I lost. It is saving me from drowningвҖҰIt is the reason I am alive today.вҖқ

Air India's Boeing 747 takes final flight from Mumbai with вҖҳwing waveвҖҷ manoeuvre.Today, one of the former Air India Boeing 747s left Mumbai for the last time. Once registered VT-EVA and named вҖңAgraвҖқ, it was de-registered by the DGCA last year after retirement, and its вҖңAir IndiaвҖқ titles and logos were removed. Hence a temporary American registration, N940AS was applied to it last week, to permit it to fly out of its former home base, towards an uncertain future.As the clock struck 10:47 AM, the pilots ferrying out the aircraft performed a вҖңWing WaveвҖқ, an aviation tradition reserved for retiring or concluding flights, over the skies of Santa Cruz.   747    747   queen

Meaning the price of a stock at anytime is вҖҳrightвҖҷ - no upside/downside can be accurately predicted as all info is available that the price reflects on.This is fundamentally false, says billionaire Jim Simons. рқ—” рқ—ірқҳӮрқ—ҝрқҳҒрқ—өрқ—Ірқ—ҝ рқ—Ҫрқ—өрқ—¶рқ—№рқ—јрқҳҖрқ—јрқ—Ҫрқ—өрқ—¶рқ—°рқ—®рқ—№ рқ—®рқ—»рқ—®рқ—№рқҳҶрқҳҖрқ—¶рқҳҖ рқҳҒрқ—ј рқ—ҰрқҳҒрқ—јрқ—¶рқ—°рқ—¶рқҳҖрқ—ә, рқ—Ўрқ—¶рқ—өрқ—¶рқ—№рқ—¶рқҳҖрқ—ә, рқ—ҳрқҳ…рқ—¶рқҳҖрқҳҒрқ—Ірқ—»рқҳҒрқ—¶рқ—®рқ—№рқ—¶рқҳҖрқ—ә & рқ—”рқ—ҜрқҳҖрқҳӮрқ—ҝрқ—ұрқ—¶рқҳҖрқ—әLife is meaningless. A very common term I've used on my account right? And there's a lot of analysis based on it as well. But how do the philosophical perspectives respond to the statement's rationality?вҖўStoicism: Even if forgotten, a virtuous life holds intrinsic value. Focus on what you can control and find meaning in the present moment.вҖўNihilism: Embrace the lack of inherent meaning and create your own through your unique experiences and actions.вҖўExistentialism: Take responsibility for defining your purpose and create meaning through your choices and connections.вҖўAbsurdism: Accept the absurdity of a meaningless universe and find joy in living authentically despite the inevitable.Ok, this was more like a theoretical answer, but on my account we don't only focus on Philosophical Questions rather we find an optimal & practical approach to these.Stoicism is good for short term or quick decisions based on the notion. You're stuck in traffic. Rather than getting frustrated, you could use the time to listen to some music or a calming audiobook.Nihilism is good for non competitive social dynamics. You break up with a partner who doesn't share your values. Now you'll seek opportunity to find someone better who actually leans with your ideas & understands your circumstances.Existentialism is the most practical for capitalistic/competitive society. Suppose you do something which barely pays you. You know that in the vast spectrum of suffering what you're doing might be insignificant, but it is what it is. You accept the truth & make your job your purpose.Absurdism is the best for hyper competitive society. You're terrified of public speaking, but you have a presentation at work. You'll accept the inherent absurdity of the situation & focus on delivering the information effectively, you'll build characteristics within you which properly delves the situation. The most rationally optimistic solution.Now ultimately it depends on you. Also I personally don't believe in a particular philosophical believe.

A very common term I've used on my account right? And there's a lot of analysis based on it as well. But how do the philosophical perspectives respond to the statement's rationality?вҖўStoicism: Even if forgotten, a virtuous life holds intrinsic value. Focus on what you can control and find meaning in the present moment.вҖўNihilism: Embrace the lack of inherent meaning and create your own through your unique experiences and actions.вҖўExistentialism: Take responsibility for defining your purpose and create meaning through your choices and connections.вҖўAbsurdism: Accept the absurdity of a meaningless universe and find joy in living authentically despite the inevitable.Ok, this was more like a theoretical answer, but on my account we don't only focus on Philosophical Questions rather we find an optimal & practical approach to these.Stoicism is good for short term or quick decisions based on the notion. You're stuck in traffic. Rather than getting frustrated, you could use the time to listen to some music or a calming audiobook.Nihilism is good for non competitive social dynamics. You break up with a partner who doesn't share your values. Now you'll seek opportunity to find someone better who actually leans with your ideas & understands your circumstances.Existentialism is the most practical for capitalistic/competitive society. Suppose you do something which barely pays you. You know that in the vast spectrum of suffering what you're doing might be insignificant, but it is what it is. You accept the truth & make your job your purpose.Absurdism is the best for hyper competitive society. You're terrified of public speaking, but you have a presentation at work. You'll accept the inherent absurdity of the situation & focus on delivering the information effectively, you'll build characteristics within you which properly delves the situation. The most rationally optimistic solution.Now ultimately it depends on you. Also I personally don't believe in a particular philosophical believe.

Story: In 2006, Joyce Vincent lived in London all by herself. Sadly, she passed away in her apartment, but no one knew for almost three years. When people finally discovered her body, her television was still playing. This story is really sad because it shows how someone could be alone for such a long time, even in a busy city like London. The TV was the only thing making any noise in her empty home all those years. It makes us think about how we can look out for and help people who might be feeling lonely or isolated. 2006,Sada's family lived in a village in Begusarai, India. It was a joint family. All the cousins of this family used to play together. One day a 6 year old girl went missing from her family but surprisingly her family members did not even tell the police about it and asked the villagers not to tell anyone. Shortly after the girl's death, another girl was born in Sada's family but after 8 months that girl also went missing. Again parents kept it as a secret for fear of being insulted even after knowing the truth. A few months later, another girl (khushi) went missing from the another family . This family member immediately called the police for investigation. During police investigation, a son of the Sada family (Amarjeet Sada) was continuously staring to the police and laughing .When the police asked Amarjeet to tell them anything, he asked for a packet of biscuits. After finding Biscuit, he took the police near dead body of khushi and said, I killed her by breaking her head with a brick and also took the blood of the previous 2 missing children of my family in the same manner. Amarjeet kept asking for biscuits again and again after telling one line. After finding out the whole truth, Amarjeet was sent to a child development centre. He was found mentally ill and released after 10 years with another identity.

2023 , Hongkong, Abby Choi was born in a very rich family. Abby herself was a successful model. Despite being very famous, Abby fell in love with a middle class boy named Alex Kong and married him. After 4 years, Abby got divorced and later in 2016 she married a rich boy named Chris Tam and even after second marriage Abby used to send money for Alex and his family. One day Abby suddenly went missing. Due to the sudden disappearance of a famous model, the Hongkong police used all their efforts to find her. The investigation revealed that Alex used to visit an old building with his family, where the police found human flesh inside the fridge and a bowl of soup . Forensic reports revealed that the soup was made from Abby's body. After strict investigation Alex told, Abby had given him an apartment worth 65 Million dollars (70 crores ) which she was going to sell and then Alex's family got the idea that if they kill Abby, then the rights of all the property will go to Alex because Abby did not have a registered or legal marriage with Tam. That's why Alex and his family met with Abby's driver and killed her. They cut Abby's body into pieces and used to make soup of it rather than decomposing it. Alex and his family members are still in police custody and waiting for their punishment.

Steak Sliders!Horseradish Mayo1/2 cup mayozest and juice of one lemon1/4 cup thinly sliced chives2 Tbsp fresh horseradish, gratedsalt and pepper to tasteFried Onions2 yellow onions, thinly sliced2 cups whole milk1 1/2 cups all purpose flour1/2 tsp salt1/2 tsp black pepper1 tsp garlic powder1 tsp paprika2 cups frying oilSteak2 Ribeye steakssalt and pepperhigh heat oil3 Tbsp unsalted butter2 sprigs rosemary2 sprigs thyme2 cloves garlic, crushedOther ingredientsSlider rollscheddar cheesefresh arugulaIn a bowl, mix mayo, lemon zest, lemon juice, chives, horseradish, salt and pepper. Mix well and place in fridge.Next, add all of your sliced onions to a bowl and cover with whole milk.In a bowl, mix together all purpose flour, salt, pepper, garlic powder and paprika. Take a handful of your onions and let the excess milk drip off. Then add it to your flour mixture and toss to coat evenly. Heat your frying oil to 350f and fry your onions in batches for 4-5 minutes or until crispy and golden brown. Then remove to paper towel lined plate and season with salt.Next, season your ribeyes generously with salt and pepper on both sides. Heat a cast iron skillet over medium high heat. Once ripping hot, add your oil and add your ribeyes. Press firmly into pan and let sear for 2-3 minutes. Then flip and move your steaks to one side of the pan. Then add your butter, thyme, rosemary and garlic. Once your butter is melted, baste your steaks until they reach 125f. Then remove to a wire lined sheet tray and let rest for at least 10 minutes.To assembleOn the bottom layer of your rolls, add a generous spoonful of horseradish mayo. Then your sliced ribeye and cheddar cheese. Melt the cheese using a broiler or kitchen torch. Then add your fried onions, arugula and more sauce. Add the top rolls, slice and enjoy!

after the Kori Gassi video I obvvsss had to do more of these. honestly i wasnвҖҷt gonna upload this as the second video but I couldnвҖҷt wait because of how this turned out haha (so stay tuned for the actual second one lolzzz)my mom is just naturally funny and the best part of most of days are talking to her about the most random things рҹҘ°so this is her recipe and as usual everyone will have their own version which may taste great but this is my moms :D (refined by me to get actual proportions for you рҹҳӮ)to make these mangaluruuuuu buns you need;- 3 large ripe bananas (riper the better)- 2 tablespoons of Greek yogurt (basically needs to have lower water content)- 3-4 tsps of sugar (adjust according to your preference and depending on how sweet your bananas are. It does become less sweet as it cooks but I found this ratio to work for me)- 1 tsp jeera (cumin) seeds- 1/2 tsp salt- 1/4 tsp baking soda- 2-3 tsp rava for some cruuunch (karkur) but this is optional- 4-5 cups of ap flour (maida) or wheat flour (atta) (I used half and half because I ran out of ap flour mid way lolz). basically keep adding till you have a sticky tacky dough- oil for greasing and fryingmix all the ingredients till combined. you should have a stickyish dough thatвҖҷs not liquidly but holds its shape and is slightly wet and tacky when you touch it.once you feel the dough is right, grease your hands and the bowl with neutral oil and place the dough in and cover. let it rest for as long as possible (also depends on the environment temperature). i let mine rest over night because I live in an igloo.once rested, sprinkle flour on your rolling surface generously and roll out poori sized buns that are thick like half an inch or so. use flour as required to avoid sticking. dust off the excess and fry in hot oil. to get it to puff up you can softly poke the bun when itвҖҷs frying (you can see it in the video) and it should bubble. fry on both sides till perfectly brown and serve hotttt with yummy coconut chutney and your day is MADE!

menceritakan seorang wanita muda yang cerdas dan suka memberontak bernama Estella (Emma Stone). Estella tinggal di Kota London, Inggris pada era 1970-an. Estella terlahir dengan rambut separuh putih dan separuh hitam yang membedakannya dengan gadis-gadis kebanyakan di dunia.Estella sejak kecil bercita-cita menjadi seorang desainer ternama. Sayangnya, impian itu kandas akibat sebuah insiden memilukan yang ia alami pada masa lalunya. Saat usianya masih 12 tahun Estella harus kehilangan kedua orang tuanya juga kehilangan sebagian hartanya dan jatuh dalam kemiskinan. Sejak itu, Estella hidup sebagai pencuri bersama dua rekan kriminalnya Horace (Paul Walter Hauser) dan Jasper (Joel Fry).Suatu ketika, impian Estella menjadi desainer terwujud dengan menjadi karyawan di sebuah rumah mode ternama The Baroness, milik desainer bertangan dingin Nyonya Baroness (Emma Thompson). Berkat bakatnya di bidang fashion, Estella ditunjuk menjadi tangan kanan Nyonya Baroness juga dipercaya mendesain koleksi The Baroness.Namun Estella adalah sosok yang tidak suka menjadi pesuruh orang lain dan menginginkan panggung untuk dirinya sendiri. Ditambah insiden yang melibatkan Nyonya Baroness dan liontin bermata merah warisan mendiang ibunya. Estella akhirnya berubah menjadi sosok berhati dingin yang tidak kenal ampun. Diapun berganti nama menjadi Cruella de Vil.Judul film/tittle/movie : Cruella (2021)

A commuter's car was vandalized by auto rickshaw drivers in Bengaluru in a case of road rage, which was captured on camera. Sharing the details on the automobile updates website Team-BHP, the man, who preferred to remain anonymous, recounted the incident that occurred at south Bengaluru's Ejipura signal during his afternoon drive for a work event.He stated that while driving at a speed of 70 to 80 kilometers per hour, he had not done anything except overtaking a few cars. At the signal, two auto rickshaws became agitated as he didn't allow them to pass and slot in behind another car. Despite his attempts to avoid confrontation, the drivers tried to intimidate him and eventually cornered him by stopping their vehicles in front of his car.The dash camera footage shared on the automotive platform captured the moment when the car window was smashed, leading to injuries for the victim. The incident gained attention after the video went viral, prompting Bengaluru Police to respond and request further details for investigation._Editor note:Dear viewers,In the video, there are two cars depicted. The first clip does not display the number plate of the targeted car; rather, it depicts how the car owner who was behind the auto was compelled to stop by obstructing his path. The second clip features the car that was actually attacked.

2008 In Amroha, Uttar Pradesh (India) at 2 o'clock in the night the villagers heard a woman (Shabnam) screaming. Shabnam was pleading for help from the balcony of her house with a very pale face. When people saw in her house they saw that the dead bodies of 7 family members of Shabnam were lying there. Shabnam told them that she did not know anything about this incident because she was sleeping on the terrace that night and had come down because of the rain. Police investigation revealed that Shabnam, along with her boyfriend (Saleem), murdered her family members because Shabnam's parents were against their marriage. That night Shabnam had given intoxicating tea to her family members then Salim went to Shabnam's house with an axe. Shabnam held her family members head and Saleem beheaded them with axe . Shabnam also choked her 10y/o nephew .Shabnam was pregnant at that time and gave birth to a child (Taj) in jail due to which the execution of Shabnam and Salim was delayed for 7 years as per Indian rules. After the court's decision, Shabnam and Saleem will be sentenced to death in Mathura jail. Their son, Taj, is even today requesting the President and the Supreme Court for relief for his parents.Shabnam will be the first woman of independent India to be hanged.

The Assam state cabinet, in a session held on Friday, endorsed the Assam Repealing Ordinance, 2024, aimed at nullifying the British-era Marriage and Divorce Act specific to Muslims. Assam's Chief Minister, Himanta Biswa Sarma, issued a resolute statement on Monday, firmly pledging to combat child marriages in the state and abolish the Assam Muslim Marriages and Divorces Registration Act, 1935, "for as long as he is alive."In a fiery address to the state assembly, Sarma criticized the Opposition's stance, affirming his determination to eradicate child marriage by 2026. He directly addressed the assembly, stating, "Hear me carefully, as long as I am alive, I will not let child marriage take place in Assam." Amidst the walkout by Congress and the All India United Democratic Front (AIUDF) protesting the cabinet's decision, Sarma issued a bold challenge, proclaiming, "I will shut down this shop before 2026."Highlighting the significance of repealing the Act, Sarma stressed its importance in providing relief to Muslim women from "torture and exploitation" while working to curb the prevalence of child marriage. He emphasized the role of the Prime Minister's initiatives in setting precedents, citing the example of triple talaq, and underscored the necessity of repealing the Act to further restrict the registration of marriages involving girls under 18 years old.Addressing the media on Friday, Sarma reiterated the importance of repealing the Act, stating, "The torture and exploitation that has been going on against Muslim mothers for so long will be ended with this bill." He emphasized the impact of repealing the Act on the ease of granting talaq and the registration of marriages involving underage girls, affirming that these measures would be significantly curtailed following the repeal.

IIT Bombay failed to hire any faculty member from Scheduled Tribe (ST) or Other Backward Class (OBC) categories last year, despite the low representation of marginalized groups in academic positions at the institute, as revealed in response to a Right to Information (RTI) query. Out of the 21 teachers hired in 2023, only four belonged to reserved categories, all from Scheduled Castes (SC), according to information obtained through an RTI request by the student group Ambedkar Periyar Phule Study Circle (APPSC). This recruitment pattern aligns with the existing caste composition of the institute's faculty, where only 6% of teachers, or 44 out of 718, come from backward communities. Students argue that this constitutes a violation of the reservation policy for central universities, which mandates a 15% quota for SCs, 7.5% for STs, and 27% for OBCs.

A timeline of a society is often marked with events that shake its conscience and do not let it forget for many years to come. A killing spree in the early 2000s by Raja Kolander is one such case. The man would not only kill people but dismember the corpse and reportedly in some cases, boil their skulls and eat their brain. A journalist with a Hindi daily, Dhirendra Singh went missing on December 14, 2000. He had left his office for home but never reached home, as per an Amar Ujala report. When he did not return even after two days, police started a search. Going through Dhirendra's call records, the police zeroed in on a phone number that belonged to the son of a Parliamentarian, the report added.The police reached the MPвҖҷs son and found the location from where the call was made. Swinging into action, the then Station House Officer of PrayagrajвҖҷs Kydganj police station, SN Tripathi, raided a pig farm in Naini and found Raja Kolander. According to a Navbharat Times report, Kolander called Dhirendra to his farmhouse on a cold winter night in December. Wearing an overcoat, the journalist arrived at the venue where a feast was awaiting him. The two men indulged in some conversation at the lavish farmhouse. Kolander asked Dhirendra to go near the fireplace as the weather was cold. During the course of interrogation, Kolander revealed that he had lent Rs 50,000 to one Kali Prasad Srivastava, also an employee at the ordnance factory. While Kolander was awaiting his repayment, Srivastava would offer him advice on how to dodge the police after committing a crime, which left an impression on Kolander, as per the Amar Ujala report. Kolander separated the skull from the body, boiled it, and drank the soup hoping to get a smart brain like that of his smart lender. Initially, Kolander and Vaksharaj were sent to Naini jail but were shifted to Lucknow jail later. Currently, both of them are serving their sentence in an Unnao jail in Uttar Pradesh.

Meme History & Origin:-The вҖңye gendu generation haiвҖқ meme originated from a speech delivered by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on the occasion of IndiaвҖҷs 75th Independence Day on August 15, 2021. In his speech, Modi praised the current generation, referring to them as the вҖңcan-doвҖқ generation, highlighting their immense potential and capabilities. However, due to a mishearing or misinterpretation, the phrase вҖңcan-doвҖқ was mistakenly heard as вҖңgendu,вҖқ which translates to вҖңstupidвҖқ in Hindi. As a result, the meme template emerged, depicting Modi stating, вҖңye gendu generation hai,вҖқ implying that this is a generation of foolish individuals. The meme is commonly used to convey the idea that the current generation lacks wisdom or intelligence, while the person sharing the meme considers themselves to be more knowledgeable or experienced.This meme is from the a speech given by modiji on 15August, 2021. ItвҖҷs a hearty meal where bite-sized pieces of spiced wheat flour dough sheets are simmered in a tangy & slightly sweet lentil soup flavored with various spices. ItвҖҷs often garnished with cilantro, onions, tomatoes, lemon juice and served hot, making it a comforting and wholesome dish. ItвҖҷs easy to think of this as the Indian version of pasta, but itвҖҷs actually a traditional Gujarati preparation that is fully packed with flavor and just happens to be vegan...

THE ONLY CASE RECORDED AS PARANORMAL IN SPANISH POLICE FILES: THE VALLECAS CASEThe Vallecas Case is SpainвҖҷs most famous incident involving paranormal activities that occurred in an apartment in Madrid.The focal point of the incident is a young girl named Estefania Gutierrez Lazaro, who had an interest in supernatural events.Estefania and two friends attempted to communicate with unseen entities using a Ouija board at school.Despite a teacherвҖҷs intervention breaking the board, EstefaniaвҖҷs friends reported seeing smoke emanate from it, which Estefania then inhaled.Following this event, changes were observed in EstefaniaвҖҷs behavior and health; she experienced insomnia, hallucinations, and epileptic seizures.Estefania claimed that faceless, cloaked figures visited her at night, wanting to take her away.Doctors could not find a medical explanation for EstefaniaвҖҷs experiences.EstefaniaвҖҷs condition worsened, and on July 13, 1991, she attacked her sister, had a catatonic seizure, and fell into a coma.She died in the hospital the next day; the autopsy report recorded her death as вҖҳsudden and suspiciousвҖҷ.After EstefaniaвҖҷs death, paranormal activities increased in her familyвҖҷs home; they experienced screams, laughter, and self-opening doors.Mrs. Gutierrez felt physical contact in the house and witnessed EstefaniaвҖҷs photograph burning one night; the rest of the house remained undamaged.The family called the police on November 27; Inspector Jose Negri and his team arrived at the house and encountered paranormal events.The police report detailed a wardrobe door opening violently, noises from the balcony, brown sludge, and a cross found on the floor.Next to the removed cross, three claw marks were found on the wall.The police stated they could not intervene in paranormal incidents and the family left the house.Mrs. Gutierrez sought psychological treatment following the events.The Vallecas Case is the first вҖҳparanormalвҖҷ incident recorded in Spanish police files.EstefaniaвҖҷs death and the subsequent events attracted significant interest in Spain and worldwide, igniting debates on paranormal activities.

Often we experience an internal conflict between what our heart desires and what our mind thinks. This conflict becomes particularly pronounced when it comes to fulfilling our desires.When the brain and heart work differently, there is some tension. The brain can analyze the situation rationally, taking into account various factors and consequences, while the heart may draw us towards something that seems emotionally appealing to us.This often leads to disagreements and struggles between our rational decisions and emotional impulses. But sometimes, when we neglect one of these aspects, we may find that we do not experience complete satisfaction from fulfilling our desires.It is important to learn to listen to and understand both the brain and the heart, and strive for a balance between them. This will allow us to make more conscious and satisfying decisions when it comes to fulfilling our desires.Still, the most effective way is through meditation practice, which can help calm the mind, gather thoughts, and make a decision that suits you best.

рҹӣ‘STOP USING THE WORD вҖҳOCDвҖҷ SO CASUALLYрҹӣ‘Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD) isnвҖҷt just about being super neat.ItвҖҷs like having a brain stuck on worry mode. Everyday tasks become stress zones, and to ease the anxiety, people do rituals or routines.ItвҖҷs like your brain stuck on repeat with worries about doing things вҖңjust right.вҖқ Imagine feeling you have to touch a doorknob a specific number of times or count your steps in precise sequences. Picture the distress of fearing harm will come to loved ones if you donвҖҷt perform certain actions. OCD can turn ordinary routines into anxiety-filled challenges.ItвҖҷs about these nagging thoughts insisting on specific actions, times, or ways, creating a constant struggle within the mind. Imagine your thoughts are storm clouds, and the rituals are attempts to clear the skies.So, next time you hear about OCD, think beyond the tidy surface вҖ“ itвҖҷs a struggle to find calm in a chaotic mind.     awareness

Neither they can be in India nor reach Nepal.At that point, Bollywood actor Suniel Shetty took matters into his own hands. Zero electricity bill, say what?I was speaking to my brother and he told me that he hasnвҖҷt been paying electricity bill at all from the last 2 years because of installing solar panels. Solar Panels are becoming increasingly popular in India but one of the problems faced is the installation cost of the same.To solve just this, the government has launched the Har Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana which is a welcome move!How to apply for PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana?-Visit the official website of PM Suryaghar: pmsuryaghar.gov.in-Click on Apply for Rooftop Solar.- In the next step, people need to register themselves by entering details like State, District, Consumer Account Number, and Electricity Distribution Company.-Then select the option to apply for the Rooftop Solar as per the form.-Now, applicants have to wait for the feasibility approval from DISCOM. Only those who have received approval from DISCOM can install the plant by any of the registered vendors in your DISCOM.-Once installation is over, submit the plant details and apply for a net meter.-After the installation of the net meter and inspection by DISCOM, people will generate a commissioning certificate from the portal.-After receiving the commissioning report, people have to submit the bank account details and a cancelled cheque through the portal to receive a subsidy within 30 days.вҖңIn order to further sustainable development and peopleвҖҷs wellbeing, we are launching the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. This project, with an investment of over Rs. 75,000 crores, aims to light up 1 crore households by providing up to 300 units of free electricity every month.вҖқ - Narendra ModiSource: Livemint, Official Portal of Muft Bijli Yojana

Jalaun: In a shocking incident, the wife and her newborn girl of Uttar Pradesh police constable died after the man could not reach home for their treatment on time. The constable, Vikas Nirmal. who is posted at Rampura police station in Jalaun district, was allegedly not given leave by his station in-charge to take his pregnant wife to the hospital.Sensing the delay, the woman's family members then intervened and took her to the Community Health Centre of Karawali in the Mainpuri district of the state. After a few hours, the couple was blessed with a baby girl. However, the condition of the mother- daughter deteriorated a day later, which was informed to the constable. After receiving the information, he requested Station Incharge Arjun Singh for leave, which was turned down by the latter. The constable then approached Jalaun SP Iraj Raja, after which he was granted a month's leave. Unfortunately, by the time he reached home, the mother-daughter duo was declared dead.

27 August 2021, Rohtak, Haryana. One day a boy named Abhishek Malik returned home after partying with his friends and finds the dead bodies of his father, Pradeep Malik, ( a rich property dealer), mother (Babli Devi), maternal grandmother (Roshni Devi) and his younger sister(Neha) was lying dead in his house. He told this to the neighbors and then the police are called. Abhishek was very sad and depressed due to the death of his family members. After a few days, in a police interogation Abishek was unable to answer some questions and the police realize that Abhishek is the real culprit. After strict interrogation, Abhishek admits that he had committed this crime. Actually, Abhishek was a gay boy who had fallen in love with one of his male friends, Kartik Latwal. Abhishek's sister came to know about this and she told this to her father, after which her father scolded him a lot but Abhishek still met his friend secretly. One day Abhishek asked his father for some money and said that he wanted to get his gender change surgery done. This made his father very angry and after this Abhishek's entire family started treating him very rudely. Angered by this, one day Abhishek shot and killed his sister, mother,father and his maternal grandmother with his father's revolver. Abishek got arrested on 2 sept 2021.

Easement DisputeCase:- MANISHA MAHENDRA GALA vs. SHALINI BHAGWAN AVATRAMANIThe court explained two words i.e. вҖҳDominant HeritageвҖҷ and вҖҳServient HeritageвҖҷ.Dominant Heritage is the land that is to be enjoyed by the beneficiary, whereas the land on which the easement is claimed is called Servient Heritage.Section 13 of the Indian Easements Act, 1882 states about easementary rights by necessity.вҖңThe easementary right by necessity could be acquired only in accordance with Section 13 of the Indian Easement Act which provides that such easementary right would arise if it is necessary for enjoying the Dominant Heritage. In the instant case, findings have been returned not only by the appellate courts but even by the trial court that there is an alternative way to access the Dominant Heritage, which may be a little far away or longer which demolishes the easement of necessity...Thus, the GalaвҖҷs (claimant) are not entitled to any easementary right by necessity upon the disputed rasta.вҖқ, the Bench Comprising Justices Pankaj Mithal and Prashant Kumar Mishra said..........

Bengaluru experienced a remarkable celestial event known as 'Zero Shadow Day' on Wednesday 24/4/2024, during which all vertical structures in the city casted no shadow for certain minutes.The Astronomical Society of India explains that due to the Earth's axial tilt of 23.5 degrees, the Sun never directly aligns above us. This alignment causes shadows to almost disappear during 'zero shadow days', making it a rare visual treat. Cities like Chennai and Mangaluru, situated on the same latitude, also experience similar phenomena on this date but at different times. day

An 82-year-old woman was allegedly mauled to death by her pet dog pitbull in Uttar Pradesh's Lucknow. The incident took place while the woman, a retired teacher, was alone in the house in Kaiserbagh locality. Her body was later found in a poll of blood by her son.The woman was rushed to the hospital where she succumbed to injuries due to excessive loss of blood, according to a report by news agency IANS.Police said the deceased woman with her 25-year-old son, a gym trainer. They own two pet dogs -- a pit bull and a Labrador. The neighbours told the police that when the son was away on work, they heard the dogs barking and the cries of Savitri."When we heard the old lady crying for help, we rushed out to their door but it was locked from inside and aunty was lying in a pool of blood. We tried to open the door but it was locked. We immediately informed her son," they said.The son rushed back home and with the help of neighbours, took his mother on a two-wheeler to Balrampur hospital from where she was referred to the KGMU trauma centre. Senior doctors at the trauma centre said that the woman had multiple deep wounds from neck to abdomen and her legs.The dog had sunk its teeth in the body of the deceased and the abdomen flesh was torn apart. Senior doctors said they tried their best but could not save the woman who succumbed due to excessive blood loss. The post-mortem examination was conducted later in the evening. The incident spread panic in the area. Locals said that the two dogs have been with the family for the past three years but never saw them turning hostile. It is not yet known what made the dog so hostile.

Sam Pitroda, chairman of the Indian Overseas Congress, has responded to the controversy surrounding his remarks on inheritance tax by saying that his comments were misconstrued to divert attention from core issues. The clarification follows criticism from Bharatiya Janata Party leaders over Pitroda's advocacy for an inheritance tax law in India.The Congress party distanced itself from Pitroda's stance, stating that his views do not always represent the party's position. Pitroda, in an interview with news agency ANI, reportedly highlighted the need for a policy on wealth redistribution and discussed the concept of inheritance tax prevalent in the United States. He explained that in the U.S., a portion of an individual's wealth is taxed upon inheritance, with approximately 55% going to the government and 45% to the heirs.Pitroda expressed his support for such a system, believing it to be a fair way to ensure that wealth is not solely retained within families but also benefits the public.

Desire is suffering; it is a contract that you make with yourself that you will be unhappy until you get what you want.
